message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
Review staff app code quality:

1. Component architecture patterns
2. State management (Context, hooks)
3. API integration and error handling
4. Code organization and reusability
5. Performance issues (unnecessary re-renders)

Check staff-app/src for code quality issues. Return findings.
</teammate-message>

<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"1","subject":"Developer Review of Staff App","description":"Code review: component architecture, state management, API integration, error handling, code organization, reusability, performance","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-02-10T18:44:38.015Z"}
</teammate-message>

content.content:
1→import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
     2→import {
     3→  View,
     4→  Text,
     5→  TouchableOpacity,
     6→  StyleSheet,
     7→  Dimensions,
     8→  Animated,
     9→  Modal,
    10→} from 'react-native';
    11→import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
    12→
    13→const { width: SCREEN_WIDTH, height: SCREEN_HEIGHT } = Dimensions.get('window');
    14→
    15→// Tab bar measurements (approximate for this tab bar design)
    16→const TAB_BAR_HEIGHT = 80;
    17→const TAB_ITEM_WIDTH = SCREEN_WIDTH / 4;
    18→
    19→export interface TourStep {
    20→  id: string;
    21→  tabIndex: number; // 0, 1, 2, 3 for the 4 tabs
    22→  title: string;
    23→  description: string;
    24→  iconName: keyof typeof Ionicons.glyphMap;
    25→}
    26→
    27→interface GuidedTourProps {
    28→  visible: boolean;
    29→  steps: TourStep[];
    30→  currentStepIndex: number;
    31→  onClose: () => void;
    32→  onNext: () => void;
    33→}
    34→
    35→// Tour steps explaining each tab
    36→export const DEFAULT_TOUR_STEPS: TourStep[] = [
    37→  {
    38→    id: '1',
    39→    tabIndex: 0,
    40→    title: 'Dashboard',
    41→    description: 'View your daily overview, attendance, schedule, and quick actions',
    42→    iconName: 'home',
    43→  },
    44→  {
    45→    id: '2',
    46→    tabIndex: 1,
    47→    title: 'Timetable',
    48→    description: 'Check your class schedule and manage substitutions',
    49→    iconName: 'calendar-clear',
    50→  },
    51→  {
    52→    id: '3',
    53→    tabIndex: 2,
    54→    title: 'Chat',
    55→    description: 'Communicate with parents, students, and staff members',
    56→    iconName: 'chatbubbles',
    57→  },
    58→  {
    59→    id: '4',
    60→    tabIndex: 3,
    61→    title: 'Profile',
    62→    description: 'Manage your profile, view payslips, and apply for leave',
    63→    iconName: 'person-circle',
    64→  },
    65→];
    66→
    67→export default function GuidedTour({
    68→  visible,
    69→  steps,
    70→  currentStepIndex,
    71→  onClose,
    72→  onNext,
    73→}: GuidedTourProps) {
    74→  const [fadeAnim] = useState(new Animated.Value(0));
    75→  const [slideAnim] = useState(new Animated.Value(50));
    76→  
    77→  const currentStep = steps[currentStepIndex];
    78→  const isLastStep = currentStepIndex === steps.length - 1;
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    if (visible && currentStep) {
    82→      // Reset animations
    83→      fadeAnim.setValue(0);
    84→      slideAnim.setValue(50);
    85→      
    86→      // Animate in
    87→      Animated.parallel([
    88→        Animated.timing(fadeAnim, {
    89→          toValue: 1,
    90→          duration: 300,
    91→          useNativeDriver: true,
    92→        }),
    93→        Animated.timing(slideAnim, {
    94→          toValue: 0,
    95→          duration: 300,
    96→          useNativeDriver: true,
    97→        }),
    98→      ]).start();
    99→    }
   100→  }, [visible, currentStepIndex, currentStep]);
   101→
   102→  const handleNext = () => {
   103→    // Animate out
   104→    Animated.parallel([
   105→      Animated.timing(fadeAnim, {
   106→        toValue: 0,
   107→        duration: 200,
   108→        useNativeDriver: true,
   109→      }),
   110→      Animated.timing(slideAnim, {
   111→        toValue: 50,
   112→        duration: 200,
   113→        useNativeDriver: true,
   114→      }),
   115→    ]).start(() => {
   116→      onNext();
   117→    });
   118→  };
   119→
   120→  const handleClose = () => {
   121→    Animated.timing(fadeAnim, {
   122→      toValue: 0,
   123→      duration: 200,
   124→      useNativeDriver: true,
   125→    }).start(() => {
   126→      onClose();
   127→    });
   128→  };
   129→
   130→  if (!currentStep) return null;
   131→
   132→  // Calculate position for the highlight (centered on the tab item)
   133→  const highlightX = currentStep.tabIndex * TAB_ITEM_WIDTH + TAB_ITEM_WIDTH / 2;
   134→
   135→  return (
   136→    <Modal visible={visible} transparent animationType="none">
   137→      <View style={styles.container}>
   138→        {/* Dark overlay with cutout for the highlighted tab */}
   139→        <View style={styles.overlay}>
   140→          {/* Top dark area */}
   141→          <View style={styles.topOverlay} />
   142→          
   143→          {/* Middle section with cutout */}
   144→          <View style={styles.middleSection}>
   145→            {/* Left dark area */}
   146→            <View style={[styles.sideOverlay, { width: highlightX - 35 }]} />
   147→            
   148→            {/* Transparent cutout area */}
   149→            <View style={styles.cutout}>
   150→              {/* Glow effect around the highlighted tab */}
   151→              <View style={styles.glowRing}>
   152→                <View style={styles.iconContainer}>
   153→                  <Ionicons 
   154→                    name={currentStep.iconName} 
   155→                    size={32} 
   156→                    color="#E31C5F" 
   157→                  />
   158→                </View>
   159→              </View>
   160→              {/* Pulsing animation indicator */}
   161→              <View style={styles.pulseIndicator} />
   162→            </View>
   163→            
   164→            {/* Right dark area */}
   165→            <View style={[styles.sideOverlay, { flex: 1 }]} />
   166→          </View>
   167→          
   168→          {/* Bottom dark area */}
   169→          <View style={styles.bottomOverlay} />
   170→        </View>
   171→
   172→        {/* Tooltip card - positioned above the highlighted tab */}
   173→        <Animated.View 
   174→          style={[
   175→            styles.tooltipCard,
   176→            {
   177→              opacity: fadeAnim,
   178→              transform: [{ translateY: slideAnim }],
   179→            },
   180→          ]}
   181→        >
   182→          {/* Arrow pointing down to the tab */}
   183→          <View style={styles.arrow} />
   184→          
   185→          {/* Close button */}
   186→          <TouchableOpacity style={styles.closeButton} onPress={handleClose}>
   187→            <Ionicons name="close" size={24} color="#6B7280" />
   188→          </TouchableOpacity>
   189→
   190→          {/* Icon */}
   191→          <View style={styles.tooltipIconContainer}>
   192→            <Ionicons name={currentStep.iconName} size={40} color="#E31C5F" />
   193→          </View>
   194→
   195→          {/* Title */}
   196→          <Text style={styles.title}>{currentStep.title}</Text>
   197→
   198→          {/* Description */}
   199→          <Text style={styles.description}>{currentStep.description}</Text>
   200→
   201→          {/* Footer with dots and Next button */}
   202→          <View style={styles.footer}>
   203→            {/* Pagination dots */}
   204→            <View style={styles.dotsContainer}>
   205→              {steps.map((_, index) => (
   206→                <View
   207→                  key={index}
   208→                  style={[
   209→                    styles.dot,
   210→                    index === currentStepIndex && styles.activeDot,
   211→                  ]}
   212→                />
   213→              ))}
   214→            </View>
   215→
   216→            {/* Next/Done button */}
   217→            <TouchableOpacity style={styles.nextButton} onPress={handleNext}>
   218→              <Text style={styles.nextButtonText}>
   219→                {isLastStep ? 'Done' : 'Next'}
   220→              </Text>
   221→            </TouchableOpacity>
   222→          </View>
   223→        </Animated.View>
   224→
   225→        {/* Skip button at top */}
   226→        <TouchableOpacity style={styles.skipButton} onPress={handleClose}>
   227→          <Text style={styles.skipText}>Skip Tour</Text>
   228→        </TouchableOpacity>
   229→      </View>
   230→    </Modal>
   231→  );
   232→}
   233→
   234→const styles = StyleSheet.create({
   235→  container: {
   236→    flex: 1,
   237→  },
   238→  overlay: {
   239→    ...StyleSheet.absoluteFillObject,
   240→  },
   241→  topOverlay: {
   242→    height: SCREEN_HEIGHT - TAB_BAR_HEIGHT - 20,
   243→    backgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.75)',
   244→  },
   245→  middleSection: {
   246→    flexDirection: 'row',
   247→    height: 80,
   248→  },
   249→  sideOverlay: {
   250→    backgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.75)',
   251→  },
   252→  cutout: {
   253→    width: 70,
   254→    height: 80,
   255→    backgroundColor: 'transparent',
   256→    justifyContent: 'center',
   257→    alignItems: 'center',
   258→  },
   259→  glowRing: {
   260→    width: 70,
   261→    height: 70,
   262→    borderRadius: 35,
   263→    backgroundColor: 'rgba(227, 28, 95, 0.2)',
   264→    justifyContent: 'center',
   265→    alignItems: 'center',
   266→  },
   267→  iconContainer: {
   268→    width: 56,
   269→    height: 56,
   270→    borderRadius: 28,
   271→    backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF',
   272→    justifyContent: 'center',
   273→    alignItems: 'center',
   274→    shadowColor: '#E31C5F',
   275→    shadowOffset: { width: 0, height: 0 },
   276→    shadowOpacity: 0.5,
   277→    shadowRadius: 10,
   278→    elevation: 10,
   279→  },
   280→  pulseIndicator: {
   281→    position: 'absolute',
   282→    width: 80,
   283→    height: 80,
   284→    borderRadius: 40,
   285→    borderWidth: 2,
   286→    borderColor: 'rgba(227, 28, 95, 0.4)',
   287→  },
   288→  bottomOverlay: {
   289→    flex: 1,
   290→    backgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.75)',
   291→  },
   292→  tooltipCard: {
   293→    position: 'absolute',
   294→    left: 20,
   295→    right: 20,
   296→    bottom: TAB_BAR_HEIGHT + 40,
   297→    backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF',
   298→    borderRadius: 20,
   299→    padding: 24,
   300→    shadowColor: '#000',
   301→    shadowOffset: { width: 0, height: 10 },
   302→    shadowOpacity: 0.3,
   303→    shadowRadius: 20,
   304→    elevation: 20,
   305→  },
   306→  arrow: {

1→import React, { useEffect, useState, useCallback } from 'react';
     2→import {
     3→  View,
     4→  Text,
     5→  ScrollView,
     6→  StyleSheet,
     7→  TouchableOpacity,
     8→  Alert,
     9→  SafeAreaView,
    10→  StatusBar,
    11→  Image,
    12→} from 'react-native';
    13→import { useAuth } from '../../context/AuthContext';
    14→import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';
    15→import { useTheme } from '../../context/ThemeContext';
    16→import api from '../../services/api';
    17→import LeaveApplicationModal from '../../components/LeaveApplicationModal';
    18→import SyncIndicator from '../../components/SyncIndicator';
    19→import { useStaffProfile } from '../../hooks/useStaffProfile';
    20→import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
    21→
    22→export default function ProfileScreen() {
    23→  const { user, logout } = useAuth();
    24→  const router = useRouter();
    25→  const { colors } = useTheme();
    26→  const { profile, syncStatus, refetch } = useStaffProfile();
    27→  const [leaves, setLeaves] = useState<any[]>([]);
    28→  const [showLeaveModal, setShowLeaveModal] = useState(false);
    29→
    30→  const loadLeaves = useCallback(async () => {
    31→    if (!user?.id) return;
    32→    try {
    33→      const data = await api.getTeacherLeaves(user.id, 'pending');
    34→      setLeaves(data);
    35→    } catch (error) {
    36→      console.error('Error loading leaves:', error);
    37→      Alert.alert('Error', 'Failed to load leave requests. Please try again.');
    38→    }
    39→  }, [user?.id]);
    40→
    41→  useEffect(() => {
    42→    loadLeaves();
    43→  }, [loadLeaves]);
    44→
    45→  const handleLogout = useCallback(() => {
    46→    Alert.alert('Logout', 'Are you sure you want to logout?', [
    47→      { text: 'Cancel', style: 'cancel' },
    48→      {
    49→        text: 'Logout',
    50→        style: 'destructive',
    51→        onPress: logout,
    52→      },
    53→    ]);
    54→  }, [logout]);
    55→
    56→  return (
    57→    <SafeAreaView style={[styles.container, { backgroundColor: colors.backgroundGray }]}>
    58→      <StatusBar barStyle={colors.background === '#FFFFFF' ? 'dark-content' : 'light-content'} backgroundColor={colors.background} />
    59→      <ScrollView>
    60→        {/* Profile Header */}
    61→        <View style={[styles.header, { backgroundColor: colors.backgroundGray }]}>
    62→          <View style={[styles.avatarContainer, { backgroundColor: colors.primary + '20', shadowColor: colors.primary }]}>
    63→            {profile?.picture ? (
    64→              <Image
    65→                source={{ uri: profile.picture }}
    66→                style={styles.avatarImage}
    67→                resizeMode="cover"
    68→              />
    69→            ) : (
    70→              <Text style={[styles.avatarText, { color: colors.primary }]}>
    71→                {user?.name?.charAt(0).toUpperCase()}
    72→              </Text>
    73→            )}
    74→          </View>
    75→          <Text style={[styles.name, { color: colors.text }]}>{user?.name}</Text>
    76→          <Text style={[styles.code, { color: colors.textSecondary }]}>{user?.code}</Text>
    77→          <View style={[styles.roleBadge, { backgroundColor: colors.primary + '20' }]}>
    78→            <Text style={[styles.role, { color: colors.primary }]}>{user?.role}</Text>
    79→          </View>
    80→
    81→          {/* Sync Indicator */}
    82→          <View style={styles.syncContainer}>
    83→            <SyncIndicator status={syncStatus} />
    84→          </View>
    85→        </View>
    86→
    87→        {/* Info Section */}
    88→        <View style={styles.section}>
    89→          <Text style={[styles.sectionTitle, { color: colors.text }]}>Personal Information</Text>
    90→          <View style={[styles.card, { backgroundColor: colors.background }]}>
    91→            <InfoRow icon="mail-outline" label="Email" value={profile?.email || user?.email} colors={colors} />
    92→            <InfoRow icon="call-outline" label="Phone" value={profile?.phone || user?.phone} colors={colors} last />
    93→          </View>
    94→        </View>
    95→
    96→        {/* Class Teacher Section */}
    97→        {user?.classes && user.classes.length > 0 && (
    98→          <View style={styles.section}>
    99→            <Text style={[styles.sectionTitle, { color: colors.text }]}>Class Teacher</Text>
   100→            <View style={[styles.card, { backgroundColor: colors.background }]}>
   101→              {user.classes.map((cls: any, index: number) => (
   102→                <View key={cls.id} style={[styles.classRow, index < user.classes.length - 1 && { borderBottomWidth: 1, borderBottomColor: colors.border }]}>
   103→                  <View style={[styles.classIconContainer, { backgroundColor: colors.primary + '20' }]}>
   104→                    <Ionicons name="school-outline" size={22} color={colors.primary} />
   105→                  </View>
   106→                  <View style={styles.classInfo}>
   107→                    <Text style={[styles.className, { color: colors.text }]}>{cls.name}</Text>
   108→                    <Text style={[styles.classSection, { color: colors.textSecondary }]}>Section {cls.section}</Text>
   109→                  </View>
   110→                  <Ionicons name="chevron-forward" size={20} color={colors.textSecondary} />
   111→                </View>
   112→              ))}
   113→            </View>
   114→          </View>
   115→        )}
   116→
   117→        {/* Pending Leaves */}
   118→        {leaves.length > 0 && (
   119→          <View style={styles.section}>
   120→            <Text style={[styles.sectionTitle, { color: colors.text }]}>Pending Leave Requests</Text>
   121→            <View style={[styles.card, { backgroundColor: colors.background }]}>
   122→              {leaves.map((leave, index) => (
   123→                <View key={leave._id} style={[styles.leaveRow, index < leaves.length - 1 && { borderBottomWidth: 1, borderBottomColor: colors.border }]}>
   124→                  <View style={[styles.leaveIconContainer, { backgroundColor: colors.inputBackground }]}>
   125→                    <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={22} color="#F5A623" />
   126→                  </View>
   127→                  <View style={styles.leaveInfo}>
   128→                    <Text style={[styles.leaveType, { color: colors.text }]}>{leave.leaveType}</Text>
   129→                    <Text style={[styles.leaveDates, { color: colors.textSecondary }]}>
   130→                      {new Date(leave.startDate).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })} - {new Date(leave.endDate).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })}
   131→                    </Text>
   132→                  </View>
   133→                  <View style={[styles.leaveDaysBadge, { backgroundColor: colors.backgroundGray }]}>
   134→                    <Text style={[styles.leaveDaysText, { color: colors.textSecondary }]}>{leave.days} days</Text>
   135→                  </View>
   136→                </View>
   137→              ))}
   138→            </View>
   139→          </View>
   140→        )}
   141→
   142→        {/* Quick Actions */}
   143→        <View style={styles.section}>
   144→          <Text style={[styles.sectionTitle, { color: colors.text }]}>Quick Actions</Text>
   145→          <View style={[styles.card, { backgroundColor: colors.background }]}>
   146→            <ActionRow
   147→              icon="create-outline"
   148→              label="Edit Profile"
   149→              onPress={() => router.push('/edit-profile')}
   150→              colors={colors}
   151→            />
   152→            <ActionRow
   153→              icon="camera-outline"
   154→              label="Change Photo"
   155→              onPress={() => router.push('/profile-photo')}
   156→              colors={colors}
   157→            />
   158→            <ActionRow
   159→              icon="time-outline"
   160→              label="Apply for Leave"
   161→              onPress={() => setShowLeaveModal(true)}
   162→              colors={colors}
   163→            />
   164→            <ActionRow
   165→              icon="card-outline"
   166→              label="My Payslips"
   167→              onPress={() => router.push('/payslips')}
   168→              colors={colors}
   169→            />
   170→            <ActionRow icon="megaphone-outline" label="Announcements" colors={colors} last />
   171→          </View>
   172→        </View>
   173→
   174→        {/* Settings */}
   175→        <View style={styles.section}>
   176→          <Text style={[styles.sectionTitle, { color: colors.text }]}>Settings</Text>
   177→          <View style={[styles.card, { backgroundColor: colors.background }]}>
   178→            <ActionRow
   179→              icon="settings-outline"
   180→              label="App Settings"
   181→              onPress={() => router.push('/settings')}
   182→              colors={colors}
   183→              last
   184→            />
   185→          </View>
   186→        </View>
   187→
   188→        {/* Logout */}
   189→        <TouchableOpacity style={[styles.logoutBtn, { backgroundColor: colors.error + '20' }]} onPress={handleLogout}>
   190→          <Ionicons name="log-out-outline" size={20} color={colors.error} />
   191→          <Text style={[styles.logoutText, { color: colors.error }]}>Logout</Text>
   192→        </TouchableOpacity>
   193→
   194→        <Text style={[styles.version, { color: colors.textSecondary }]}>Staff App v1.0.0</Text>
   195→
   196→        <LeaveApplicationModal
   197→          visible={showLeaveModal}
   198→          teacherId={user?.id || ''}
   199→          onClose={() => setShowLeaveModal(false)}
   200→          onSuccess={() => {
   201→            loadLeaves();
   202→            refetch();
   203→            setShowLeaveModal(false);
   204→          }}
   205→        />
   206→      </ScrollView>
   207→    </SafeAreaView>
   208→  );
   209→}
   210→
   211→function InfoRow({ icon, label, value, colors, last }: { icon: string; label: string; value?: string; colors: any; last?: boolean }) {
   212→  return (
   213→    <View style={[styles.infoRow, !last && { borderBottomWidth: 1, borderBottomColor: colors.border }]}>
   214→      <View style={styles.infoLeft}>
   215→        <Ionicons name={icon as any} size={20} color={colors.textSecondary} style={styles.infoIcon} />
   216→        <Text style={[styles.infoLabel, { color: colors.textSecondary }]}>{label}</Text>
   217→      </View>
   218→      <Text style={[styles.infoValue, { color: colors.text }]}>{value || '-'}</Text>
   219→    </View>
   220→  );
   221→}
   222→
   223→function ActionRow({ icon, label, onPress, colors, last }: { icon: string; label: string; onPress?: () => void; colors: any; last?: boolean }) {
   224→  return (
   225→    <TouchableOpacity style={[styles.actionRow, !last && { borderBottomWidth: 1, borderBottomColor: colors.border }]} onPress={onPress}>
   226→      <View style={styles.actionLeft}>
   227→        <Ionicons name={icon as any} size={22} color={colors.text} style={styles.actionIcon} />
   228→        <Text style={[styles.actionText, { color: colors.text }]}>{label}</Text>
   229→      </View>
   230→      <Ionicons name="chevron-forward" size={20} color={colors.textSecondary} />
   231→    </TouchableOpacity>
   232→  );
   233→}

input.content:
# Staff App Code Quality Review

## 1. Component Architecture Patterns

### Strengths
- **Clean separation of concerns**: Context, hooks, services, components, and types are well-organized
- **React Router (Expo Router)**: Proper file-based routing with tabs and stack navigation
- **Custom hooks pattern**: `useStaffProfile`, `useNotifications`, `useGuidedTour` abstract complex logic
- **TypeScript usage**: Type definitions in `types/api.types.ts` are comprehensive

### Issues Found
- **Inline component definitions**: `SelfAttendance` and `ClassAttendance` components defined inside `attendance.tsx:121-266` - should be separate files
- **Giant dashboard component**: `app/(tabs)/index.tsx` is 824 lines - needs decomposition
- **Mixed component patterns**: Some screens export functions, others export default with different patterns

### Recommendations
- Extract `SelfAttendance` and `ClassAttendance` to separate component files
- Split dashboard into sub-components (`AttendanceCard`, `ScheduleCard`, `QuickActionsGrid`, etc.)
- Standardize component export patterns

---

## 2. State Management (Context, Hooks)

### Strengths
- **Well-structured contexts**: `AuthContext`, `ThemeContext`, `NotificationContext` follow proper patterns
- **Custom hooks for encapsulation**: Good use of `useCallback` and `useMemo` in contexts
- **Proper dependency management**: Most useEffect hooks have correct dependencies

### Issues Found
**AuthContext.tsx:42-73** - Error handling has nested try-catch for corrupted data but could be more robust:
```tsx
if (error?.message?.includes('Could not decrypt')) {
  // String matching is fragile
}
```

**useStaffProfile.ts:138** - Large dependency array causing potential re-renders:
```tsx
// useEffect depends on 6 callbacks - may trigger too often
}, [user?.id, fetchProfile, handleStaffUpdate, handleAuthenticated, handleDisconnected, handleConnectError]);
```

**Dashboard screen state**: Uses multiple refs to prevent duplicate loads (hasLoadedRef, isLoadingRef) - this is a code smell indicating the data fetching logic needs refactoring.

### Recommendations
- Create a centralized error handler instead of string matching
- Use reducer pattern or state machine for complex profile sync logic
- Consider React Query or SWR for data fetching to eliminate manual loading state management

---

## 3. API Integration and Error Handling

### Strengths
- **Centralized API service**: Single `api.ts` service class with timeout handling
- **Timeout implementation**: `fetchWithTimeout` method prevents hanging requests
- **Consistent error throwing**: Most methods throw on failure

### Issues Found
**Inconsistent error responses**: Some endpoints check `response.ok` differently:
```tsx
// Some methods:
if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed...');

// Others:
if (!response.ok) {
  const errorData = await response.json().catch(() => ({}));
  throw new Error(errorData.error || 'Failed...');
}
```

**No retry logic**: Network failures are not retried automatically

**Token management**: Token is cached but no auto-refresh mechanism

**Socket reconnection**: Good but disconnects after max attempts without recovery strategy

### Recommendations
- Standardize error handling across all API methods
- Implement exponential backoff retry for failed requests
- Add token refresh interceptor
- Implement offline queue for failed requests

---

## 4. Code Organization and Reusability

### Strengths
- **Barrel exports**: `components/index.ts` and `hooks/index.ts` provide clean imports
- **Type safety**: Good TypeScript coverage with proper interfaces

### Issues Found
**Duplicated logic**:
- Color interpolation with `+ '15'` opacity repeated throughout: `colors.primary + '15'`
- Empty state rendering patterns duplicated

**Magic strings**:
```tsx
const DAYS = ['Monday', 'Tuesday', 'Wednesday', 'Thursday', 'Friday', 'Saturday'];
// Hardcoded in timetable.tsx:17
```

**Inline styles** in many components instead of using StyleSheet.create (e.g., login.tsx:72-80)

### Recommendations
- Create utility functions for color opacity
- Move constants to shared config
- Use StyleSheet consistently
- Create reusable empty state component

---

## 5. Performance Issues

### Critical Issues

**Unnecessary re-renders in dashboard**:
- `loadDashboard` callback has `refreshing` in dependency array causing dependency loop
- Debug state updates in effect that was intentionally removed (commented on line 161)

**useStaffProfile hook**:
- Creates new callback references on every render due to dependency on `fetchProfile`
- Socket event handlers are recreated on each render

**Timetable screen**:
- Renders all 7 days even when only current day is needed
- No memoization of day cards

### Specific File Issues

**app/(tabs)/index.tsx:69-140**:
```tsx
const loadDashboard = useCallback(async () => {
  // ... 70 lines of logic
}, [user?.id, refreshing]); // 'refreshing' causes issues
```

**attendance.tsx:213-257**:
- Student list renders without memoization - each status update re-renders entire list

**components/GuidedTour.tsx:74-100**:
- Animation state reset on every step change could be optimized

### Recommendations
1. Use `React.memo` for list items (StudentRow, PeriodCard)
2. Implement virtual list for long timetables
3. Use `useReducer` for complex state (dashboard)
4. Move static data outside components (DAYS array, tour steps)
5. Consider using FlatList instead of ScrollView with map

---

## Summary Score

| Category | Score | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Component Architecture | 7/10 | Good structure, some large files |
| State Management | 7/10 | Well-organized contexts, some complexity |
| API Integration | 6/10 | Basic error handling, missing retry/refresh |
| Code Organization | 8/10 | Clean folder structure, minor duplication |
| Performance | 5/10 | Multiple re-render issues, no virtualization |

**Overall: 6.6/10** - Solid foundation with room for optimization.
input.summary: Staff app code quality review completed

## 2. State Management (Context, Hooks)

- **Custom hooks for encapsulation**: Good use of `useCallback` and `useMemo` in contexts
- **Proper dependency management**: Most useEffect hooks have correct dependencies

### Issues Found
**AuthContext.tsx:42-73** - Error handling has nested try-catch for corrupted data but could be more robust:
```tsx
if (error?.message?.includes('Could not decrypt')) {
  // String matching is fragile
}
```

**useStaffProfile.ts:138** - Large dependency array causing potential re-renders:
```tsx
// useEffect depends on 6 callbacks - may trigger too often
}, [user?.id, fetchProfile, handleStaffUpdate, handleAuthenticated, handleDisconnected, handleConnectError]);
```

**Dashboard screen state**: Uses multiple refs to prevent duplicate loads (hasLoadedRef, isLoadingRef) - this is a code smell indicating the data fetching logic needs refactoring.

### Recommendations
- Create a centralized error handler instead of string matching
- Use reducer pattern or state machine for complex profile sync logic
- Consider React Query or SWR for data fetching to eliminate manual loading state management

---

## 3. API Integration and Error Handling

### Strengths
- **Centralized API service**: Single `api.ts` service class with timeout handling
- **Timeout implementation**: `fetchWithTimeout` method prevents hanging requests
- **Consistent error throwing**: Most methods throw on failure

### Issues Found
**Inconsistent error responses**: Some endpoints check `response.ok` differently:
```tsx
// Some methods:
if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed...');

// Others:
if (!response.ok) {
  const errorData = await response.json().catch(() => ({}));
  throw new Error(errorData.error || 'Failed...');
}
```

**No retry logic**: Network failures are not retried automatically

**Token management**: Token is cached but no auto-refresh mechanism

**Socket reconnection**: Good but disconnects after max attempts without recovery strategy

### Recommendations
- Standardize error handling across all API methods
- Implement exponential backoff retry for failed requests
- Add token refresh interceptor
- Implement offline queue for failed requests

---

## 4. Code Organization and Reusability

### Strengths
- **Barrel exports**: `components/index.ts` and `hooks/index.ts` provide clean imports
- **Type safety**: Good TypeScript coverage with proper interfaces

### Issues Found
**Duplicated logic**:
- Color interpolation with `+ '15'` opacity repeated throughout: `colors.primary + '15'`
- Empty state rendering patterns duplicated

**Magic strings**:
```tsx
const DAYS = ['Monday', 'Tuesday', 'Wednesday', 'Thursday', 'Friday', 'Saturday'];
// Hardcoded in timetable.tsx:17
```

**Inline styles** in many components instead of using StyleSheet.create (e.g., login.tsx:72-80)

### Recommendations
- Create utility functions for color opacity
- Move constants to shared config
- Use StyleSheet consistently
- Create reusable empty state component

---

## 5. Performance Issues

### Critical Issues

**Unnecessary re-renders in dashboard**:
- `loadDashboard` callback has `refreshing` in dependency array causing dependency loop
- Debug state updates in effect that was intentionally removed (commented on line 161)

**useStaffProfile hook**:
- Creates new callback references on every render due to dependency on `fetchProfile`
- Socket event handlers are recreated on each render

**Timetable screen**:
- Renders all 7 days even when only current day is needed
- No memoization of day cards

### Specific File Issues

**app/(tabs)/index.tsx:69-140**:
```tsx
const loadDashboard = useCallback(async () => {
  // ... 70 lines of logic
}, [user?.id, refreshing]); // 'refreshing' causes issues
```

**attendance.tsx:213-257**:
- Student list renders without memoization - each status update re-renders entire list

**components/GuidedTour.tsx:74-100**:
- Animation state reset on every step change could be optimized

### Recommendations
1. Use `React.memo` for list items (StudentRow, PeriodCard)
2. Implement virtual list for long timetables
3. Use `useReducer` for complex state (dashboard)
4. Move static data outside components (DAYS array, tour steps)
5. Consider using FlatList instead of ScrollView with map

---

## Summary Score

| Category | Score | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Component Architecture | 7/10 | Good structure, some large files |
| State Management | 7/10 | Well-organized contexts, some complexity |
| API Integration | 6/10 | Basic error handling, missing retry/refresh |
| Code Organization | 8/10 | Clean folder structure, minor duplication |
| Performance | 5/10 | Multiple re-render issues, no virtualization |

**Overall: 6.6/10** - Solid foundation with room for optimization."
  }
}

routing.summary: Staff app code quality review completed
routing.content:
# Staff App Code Quality Review

## 1. Component Architecture Patterns

### Strengths
- **Clean separation of concerns**: Context, hooks, services, components, and types are well-organized
- **React Router (Expo Router)**: Proper file-based routing with tabs and stack navigation
- **Custom hooks pattern**: `useStaffProfile`, `useNotifications`, `useGuidedTour` abstract complex logic
- **TypeScript usage**: Type definitions in `types/api.types.ts` are comprehensive

### Issues Found
- **Inline component definitions**: `SelfAttendance` and `ClassAttendance` components defined inside `attendance.tsx:121-266` - should be separate files
- **Giant dashboard component**: `app/(tabs)/index.tsx` is 824 lines - needs decomposition
- **Mixed component patterns**: Some screens export functions, others export default with different patterns

### Recommendations
- Extract `SelfAttendance` and `ClassAttendance` to separate component files
- Split dashboard into sub-components (`AttendanceCard`, `ScheduleCard`, `QuickActionsGrid`, etc.)
- Standardize component export patterns

---

## 2. State Management (Context, Hooks)

### Strengths
- **Well-structured contexts**: `AuthContext`, `ThemeContext`, `NotificationContext` follow proper patterns
- **Custom hooks for encapsulation**: Good use of `useCallback` and `useMemo` in contexts
- **Proper dependency management**: Most useEffect hooks have correct dependencies

### Issues Found
**AuthContext.tsx:42-73** - Error handling has nested try-catch for corrupted data but could be more robust:
```tsx
if (error?.message?.includes('Could not decrypt')) {
  // String matching is fragile
}
```

**useStaffProfile.ts:138** - Large dependency array causing potential re-renders:
```tsx
// useEffect depends on 6 callbacks - may trigger too often
}, [user?.id, fetchProfile, handleStaffUpdate, handleAuthenticated, handleDisconnected, handleConnectError]);
```

**Dashboard screen state**: Uses multiple refs to prevent duplicate loads (hasLoadedRef, isLoadingRef) - this is a code smell indicating the data fetching logic needs refactoring.

### Recommendations
- Create a centralized error handler instead of string matching
- Use reducer pattern or state machine for complex profile sync logic
- Consider React Query or SWR for data fetching to eliminate manual loading state management

---

## 3. API Integration and Error Handling

### Strengths
- **Centralized API service**: Single `api.ts` service class with timeout handling
- **Timeout implementation**: `fetchWithTimeout` method prevents hanging requests
- **Consistent error throwing**: Most methods throw on failure

### Issues Found
**Inconsistent error responses**: Some endpoints check `response.ok` differently:
```tsx
// Some methods:
if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed...');

// Others:
if (!response.ok) {
  const errorData = await response.json().catch(() => ({}));
  throw new Error(errorData.error || 'Failed...');
}
```

**No retry logic**: Network failures are not retried automatically

**Token management**: Token is cached but no auto-refresh mechanism

**Socket reconnection**: Good but disconnects after max attempts without recovery strategy

### Recommendations
- Standardize error handling across all API methods
- Implement exponential backoff retry for failed requests
- Add token refresh interceptor
- Implement offline queue for failed requests

---

## 4. Code Organization and Reusability

### Strengths
- **Barrel exports**: `components/index.ts` and `hooks/index.ts` provide clean imports
- **Type safety**: Good TypeScript coverage with proper interfaces

### Issues Found
**Duplicated logic**:
- Color interpolation with `+ '15'` opacity repeated throughout: `colors.primary + '15'`
- Empty state rendering patterns duplicated

**Magic strings**:
```tsx
const DAYS = ['Monday', 'Tuesday', 'Wednesday', 'Thursday', 'Friday', 'Saturday'];
// Hardcoded in timetable.tsx:17
```

**Inline styles** in many components instead of using StyleSheet.create (e.g., login.tsx:72-80)

### Recommendations
- Create utility functions for color opacity
- Move constants to shared config
- Use StyleSheet consistently
- Create reusable empty state component

---

## 5. Performance Issues

### Critical Issues

**Unnecessary re-renders in dashboard**:
- `loadDashboard` callback has `refreshing` in dependency array causing dependency loop
- Debug state updates in effect that was intentionally removed (commented on line 161)

**useStaffProfile hook**:
- Creates new callback references on every render due to dependency on `fetchProfile`
- Socket event handlers are recreated on each render

**Timetable screen**:
- Renders all 7 days even when only current day is needed
- No memoization of day cards

### Specific File Issues

**app/(tabs)/index.tsx:69-140**:
```tsx
const loadDashboard = useCallback(async () => {
  // ... 70 lines of logic
}, [user?.id, refreshing]); // 'refreshing' causes issues
```

**attendance.tsx:213-257**:
- Student list renders without memoization - each status update re-renders entire list

**components/GuidedTour.tsx:74-100**:
- Animation state reset on every step change could be optimized

### Recommendations
1. Use `React.memo` for list items (StudentRow, PeriodCard)
2. Implement virtual list for long timetables
3. Use `useReducer` for complex state (dashboard)
4. Move static data outside components (DAYS array, tour steps)
5. Consider using FlatList instead of ScrollView with map

---

## Summary Score

| Category | Score | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Component Architecture | 7/10 | Good structure, some large files |
| State Management | 7/10 | Well-organized contexts, some complexity |
| API Integration | 6/10 | Basic error handling, missing retry/refresh |
| Code Organization | 8/10 | Clean folder structure, minor duplication |
| Performance | 5/10 | Multiple re-render issues, no virtualization |

**Overall: 6.6/10** - Solid foundation with room for optimization.

content.text:
I've completed the staff app code quality review. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Code Quality Review Complete

### Key Findings:

**1. Component Architecture (7/10)**
- Clean folder structure with good separation
- Dashboard component is 824 lines - needs decomposition
- Inline component definitions in attendance.tsx should be extracted

**2. State Management (7/10)**
- Well-structured contexts (Auth, Theme, Notification)
- Complex socket state management in `useStaffProfile` could use reducer pattern
- Dashboard uses multiple refs to prevent duplicate loads - indicates design smell

**3. API Integration (6/10)**
- Centralized API service with timeout handling
- Inconsistent error handling patterns across methods
- No retry logic or token refresh mechanism
- Socket disconnects after max attempts without recovery

**4. Code Organization (8/10)**
- Good TypeScript coverage
- Repeated patterns like `colors.primary + '15'` should be utilities
- Magic strings hardcoded (DAYS array)
- Inline styles instead of StyleSheet in some files

**5. Performance (5/10)**
- Unnecessary re-renders in dashboard (refreshing in dependency array)
- Student list in attendance not memoized
- Timetable renders all 7 days unnecessarily
- No virtualization for long lists

**Overall Score: 6.6/10** - Solid foundation with clear areas for improvement.
